message.content:
[
  "Insert activity 'add respondent bank to blacklist' inside the loop branch 'sequence (cond: !data.evaluation)' immediately before 'Lookup Partner Banks'. This ensures that whenever 'conduct due diligence study' causes 'not data.evaluation' to hold, the blacklist activity eventually executes. Keep the existing post-parallel exclusive blacklist branch unchanged. This is localized and preserves R1 and R3.",
  "Insert activity 'add respondent bank to blacklist' inside the loop branch 'sequence (cond: !data.evaluation)' immediately after 'Lookup Partner Banks'. This resolves the violation for cases where 'not data.evaluation' becomes true during due diligence, while preserving the existing later blacklist handling. This may delay blacklisting until after partner-bank lookup but still satisfies eventual-follow semantics.",
  "Move the existing post-parallel exclusive blacklist decision into the loop branch that handles failed evaluation: replace the inner branch 'sequence (cond: !data.evaluation) Lookup Partner Banks' with 'sequence (cond: !data.evaluation) add respondent bank to blacklist; Lookup Partner Banks', and remove the later exclusive block that blacklists on 'not data.evaluation'. This avoids duplicate blacklist execution and ensures the blacklist action follows the condition as soon as it is detected, while preserving R1 and R3.",
  "Strengthen the existing post-parallel exclusive block so that 'add respondent bank to blacklist' occurs on all paths where 'not data.evaluation' may have become true: replace the branch 'sequence (cond: not (not data.evaluation))' with a sequence that first checks whether a failed evaluation was observed during due diligence and, if so, executes 'add respondent bank to blacklist'. This is a partial-compliance strategy unless the process explicitly stores a persistent flag for prior failed evaluation states, because the final value of 'data.evaluation' alone may no longer reflect that the condition was previously true."
]
